title: leontodon hastilis subsp. alpinus
intro: Leontodon hastilis subsp. alpinus, also known as mountain hawk's beard or mountain sow thistle, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the high-altitude regions of the European Alps. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been employed in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Leontodon hastilis subsp. alpinus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
